summary: Renamed the widely used placeholder angle function `func_someCalcAngle_80022C30` to the standard math name `atan2f` across C sources, assembly callsites, and headers. The PR body says `atan2f` was matched in a decomp.me scratch and split out because there were many occurrences; it also notes a related `atanf` match and dependency on PR #434. The change centralized related lbrefract/trig prototypes in a new `src/melee/lb/lbrefract.h`, removed the old declaration from `include/functions.h`, and updated includes in users such as `lbvector.h`, `fighter.h`, and `mtx.h`.
what_changed: Replaced many direct calls and assembly branch labels from `func_someCalcAngle_80022C30` to `atan2f` while preserving the same two-argument float call pattern.
Removed `f32 func_someCalcAngle_80022C30(f32, f32); // atan2` and nearby unresolved lbrefract-related declarations from `include/functions.h`.
Added `src/melee/lb/lbrefract.h` with prototypes for nearby 0x80022xxx functions, including `extern f32 atan2f(f32 y, f32 x);`, `func_80022D1C`, `func_80022DBC`, and `func_80022E68`.
Updated `lbvector.c` to call `atan2f` in `lbvector_EulerAnglesFromONB` and removed local extern declarations for the old angle function and related trig helpers.
Updated `lbvector.h`, `mtx.h`, and `fighter.h` to include `melee/lb/lbrefract.h` instead of carrying scattered externs.
Renamed parameters in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/mtx.c` helper `calcVal` from generic `val1`/`val2` to `x`/`y`, matching `atan2f(y, x)` semantics.
Propagated the rename through fighter code handling movement, knockback, specials, ground normals, stick angles, projectile angles, and visual effect orientations.
Propagated the rename through many remaining assembly-only files, including camera, item, fighter, ground, interface, text, and baselib assembly callsites.
smart_moves: Split a high-fanout rename into its own PR, as the PR body explicitly notes there were many occurrences.
Used the standard library-style name `atan2f` rather than a descriptive project placeholder, improving readability at every callsite involving vector or stick angle calculation.
Kept argument names in the new prototype as `y` and `x`, making the non-intuitive `atan2f(y, x)` ordering explicit for future C rewrites.

Moved related prototypes out of the global `include/functions.h` catch-all into `src/melee/lb/lbrefract.h`, reducing global header noise and giving this 0x80022xxx math/refract group a more specific home.
Updated both C and assembly callsites in one sweep, avoiding mixed symbol names that would make searches and future decomp matching harder.
Cleaned up redundant local extern declarations in `lbvector.c`, `lbvector.h`, and `mtx.h` once a proper header existed.
decomp_lessons: When a placeholder function is conclusively identified as a standard math primitive, prefer the canonical name (`atan2f`) over project-specific descriptions like `someCalcAngle`.
For common math functions with many callsites, a repository-wide rename is valuable even before every caller is decompiled because it documents semantics in assembly and C simultaneously.
Prototype argument names can encode important semantic ordering; `atan2f(f32 y, f32 x)` prevents accidental reversal when converting assembly or refactoring C.
Do not leave matched library functions only in `include/functions.h`; group nearby library or subsystem prototypes into a dedicated header and include it where needed.
Matched primitives such as `atan2f`, `asin`, and `acos` often unlock more readable vector code, euler angle reconstruction, collision-normal handling, and fighter movement logic.
A rename-only PR can still improve future matching by making callsite intent obvious and by replacing comments like `// atan2` with an actual symbol name.

naming_conventions.lesson: Use the canonical C/math function name once the function is matched and semantics are known.
Replace explanatory comments on placeholder names with a real symbol and meaningful parameter names.
Name local helper parameters to match geometric/math roles, especially when feeding `atan2f(y, x)`.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR body cites decomp.me scratches for matched `atan2f` and `atanf`; only the `atan2f` rename is directly evidenced by the diff excerpt.
Assembly callsites were updated mechanically from `bl func_someCalcAngle_80022C30` to `bl atan2f` without changing instruction bytes or control flow.
The broad callsite pattern in assembly and C shows the identified function returns an angle from two float components, often ground normal, velocity, or stick vector components.
For future matching, search for existing `atan2f(y, x)` callsites when reconstructing angle-from-vector code rather than reintroducing placeholder externs.

The `mtx.c` helper preserves a special case around near-zero `x` using `fabsf_bitwise(x) <= FLOAT_MIN`, then falls back to `atan2f(y, x)`; this is a useful pattern for matching matrix-to-angle extraction code.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the supplied PR slice.
The only stated rationale is from the PR body: `atan2f` was matched externally and the rename was split into its own PR because of the large number of occurrences.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename `func_80022D1C`, `func_80022DBC`, or `func_80022E68` to standard math functions.
Search for remaining placeholder calls that are comments-only identified as standard math functions in `include/functions.h`.
Check PR #434 for the dependency that enabled this rename and any associated matching or header setup.
Search current tree for `atan2f(` usage to reuse argument ordering and include patterns in future decompilation.
Investigate whether `src/melee/lb/lbrefract.h` remained the long-term home for these prototypes or was later reorganized.
